title: Don’t Make These Mistakes When Buying Olive Oil

chunks.title: Importance and Quality of Olive Oil
chunks.summary: The video discusses the health benefits of olive oil and emphasizes the importance of its quality, including factors like being cold pressed and single origin. It also warns about the presence of harmful phthalates in some olive oils, making it crucial to choose organic and glass containers.

chunks.title: Contamination Concerns in Oils
chunks.summary: The speaker highlights the contamination of olive and avocado oils with phthalates, leading to health risks. Consumers are encouraged to research oil quality to avoid products with high levels of these endocrine disruptors.
chunks.transcript: so basically olive oils can be good but you have to know are they high in phalates and there are some that are much lower and some that are very high so is it kind of one of the things of like with olive oil you get what you pay for like you might not necessarily oh really okay so it doesn't you don't have to like spend a ton you don't necessarily have to spend a ton it's probably essentially the same cost for a good versus a poor olive oil so we were in Sprouts yesterday actually planning this YouTube and this reel out and we were looking avocado oils also and there's one avocado oil it's the mava Mad haa that had a huge amount of phades in it and it had like 50,000 parts per billion of phades which is which is a large number relative to what other avocado had so yes an avocado oil is kind of like an olive oil it's better than a seed oil but when you're doing all those things it's important to know like okay even the olive oils and avocado oils can be contaminated with endocrine disrupting chemicals so just go the one little step for yourself and your family and educate yourself and say okay this test was done um and this one is lower in these phalates if I'm going

chunks.title: Cooking Oils: Recommendations
chunks.summary: The recommendation shifts to cooking oils, stating that olive and avocado oils should not be heated due to their instability at high temperatures. Instead, tallow is suggested as a better cooking fat, with alternatives like ghee for those who need a higher smoke point.
chunks.transcript: to use it the other thing that I'll add is just an asterisk on the conversation with olive and avocado is I don't think they should be heated these are essentially salad dressing oils or just topping oils if you want to put it on whatever like a snack or something people put it on like Bretta or something you know that it's a topping oil these oils are less stable at heat than an animal fat which is more highly saturated so what should we be cooking in instead so I think that Tallow is probably the best thing to cooking butter is okay but it will burn because of the milk solids that's where you get into ghee if the milk solids have been removed it's going to burn at a lower temperature at a higher temperature so the idea here is the more saturated the oil is the more stable it is for cooking
